author: @happytoastytoaster
text: Just got mine in, looked for an unboxing vid, and here I am, RIP Rob😢

author: @pnaspnas
text:
I literally just bought a Saturn printer and ended up in Robert’s videos tonight. He explained everything in such a calm, colorful way that I immediately thought “this is the kind of older guy I’d love to be friends with.”

Then I clicked over and heard your explanation of what happened and it honestly punched me in the chest. I’m so sorry you lost your brother, and that he lost Patti and his mobility.

For what it’s worth, his energy isn’t gone. A complete stranger in another country got his printer set up tonight because of him, and I’ll think of him every time I use it.

author: @pnaspnas
text: Thank you very helpful and colorful explanation

author: @Emtbtoday
text: I'm resin 3D printing or nothing. I have been using Elegoo printers for the past 5+ years now. I just took delivery of the 16k Saturn 4 Ultra today! Like you said about habits, this is second nature to me now, and you find your own ways of using these and how to keep your workspace spotless in the process! You wouldn't believe it until you experience it, only another resin 3D printer user will understand that everyone makes a huge fuss over spilled resin with these printers. Honestly, the smell of melting plastic from filament printers would be worse, in my opinion, as these resin printers don't smell like they used to! Resin and filament is like an Apple Samsung thing haha always trying to prove what's better and resin being the Samsung and filament Apple and we all know Samsung win everytime 😂

author: @HauptlingHD
text: You shouldn't show your Wi-Fi SSID in a Video. Especially if you show more SSIDs in the vicinity at the same time. Your exact location can be easily triangulated with this information. I highly encourage you to use the edit feature of YouTube, if you have access to it, and cut out or pixelate that section or re-upload/replace this Video with one that doesn't show the Wi-Fi networks around you.

text:
The nightmare begins when I bought the new machine - ELEGOO Saturn 4 Ultra 16K ! The first print stopped automatically at 86%! Customer service said it was a software update issue. Then I found that the protective film had bubbles in the middle - it wasn't applied properly at the factory! After replacing it, during the third print, the release film tore even more severely, causing resin to leak onto the LED screen, which damaged the LCD screen. Now I have to spend ¥730 to buy a new one!
新机买回来是恶梦的开始！打印第一次去到86%自动停了！客服说软件更新问题。最后发现钢化膜中间起了泡，出厂贴不好！更换后，打第三次离型膜更加穿了，树脂流到LED屏，导致LCD屏幕损坏，现在又要花¥730重新购买！

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: The Elegood Saturn 4 ultra 16k has literally just come out mate - you couldn't buy that before today - so you can't mean this machine

replies.author: @gavinlynas2833
replies.text: Need need. I plan on 3d printing very thin circuit board like stencils and using a liquid graphene concoction to fill in the gaps. You can either paint it onto something or leave it in inbedded as an actual circuit board. Then just paint over the circuit lines with something nonconductive to prevent any shorting.

author: @beautifulsmall
text: After buying a set of gear cutters for a project , i looked at PCBWAY and uploaded my hub gear to find a price of £1.65 per part, ordered 5 with cheap postage , total under £10, they are amazingly good, within 0.1mm and 0.05mm ovalicity. UTR8220 resin. ( downside, delivery 2 weeks )   Looking on the Elgoo website I don't see a wavelength of the UV, or if it can print with othe resins, maybe they are all the same ?  Would love to see how the Elegoo prints small gears.  The price is half the recently taboo Bambu. Looking forward to seeing your prints.

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: Also, I get the water washable resins. A nice 5 gallon bucket partly filled. Print, shake rinse, towel dry and window cure. One thing to consider, the darker the resin, the longer the build exposures. For black, the factory 2 didn't do. I use 5 to 7 sec exposures. And 30 sec on the bottom build plate attachment layer. If a second build plate and revivor were supplied, I could just keep printing while cleaning.

replies.author: @Brenners2010
replies.text: Are there any downsides to using the water washable resins?

replies.author: @stewartpalmer2456
replies.text: @Brenners2010 You still have to allow the water to evaporate for environmental reasons. But it's cheaper then alcohol. Other then that I can't find any.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome thanks for the tips on black mate

replies.author: @Brenners2010
replies.text: @stewartpalmer2456 can you dab the surface with kitchen paper to dry it?

replies.author: @stewartpalmer2456
replies.text: @Brenners2010 I would use lint free clothes or rags. I wouldn't use paper.

author: @CrudelyMade
text:
I thing to note is that the 4k, 8k, 16k...   is not a comprehensible rating. it's like saying a tv is 30 inch or 50 inch.. or that it 4k or 8k...  but the real value measurement is PPI  "pixels per inch".
which you can calculate.  on TV it will tell you how detailed the picture is (an 8k 30 inch has different detail levels than an 8k 50 inch)..  
and for resin printers it's the same. you have to look at the square footage of the build plate and compare against the pixels of the projection to get 'pixels per inch'.. 
another way to do is it their listed pixel size.   after that, getting into minutae, is the shape of the pixel, but really.. unless you're under a microscope, you're unlikely to notice any differences at the higher pixel per inch resolutions.   some miniature painters might also notice..  and sometimes lower res printers did better jobs than higher res because in higher res you could see layer lines in some orientations...   
but yeah..  mostly 'pixels per inch' and 'build volume' are the two major things to look at.  
the new features like peeling the part, resin feeding etc aside..  the above is just about the '4k/8k/16k' labels  that are often pushed because it's a simple number people can latch onto. 
otherwise, good for you! (for recognizing your own self imposed limitations and overcoming them).

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and thanks for taking the time to post the info

author: @FuhrChris
text:
I can give you the Run down skinny on "LEARNING" Zbrushing when you are ready.
(One of the current problems is that Zbrush has issues when it comes to making dimensioned parts, so they normally run the low Polly part through other software to get a precise demotion.)

(Here in the States' we don't have as much EXPOSURE to the Golden Age development of the  Victorians' in which parts and entire building housed machines were encrusted with Sculpted and Cast machinery Parts that added up to a Theme that runs though ALL of it.  I have been forecasting a return the embellished designs.  
You on the other hand can travel to places with Victorian Decorative Iron Works.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate
